proposed_from.topic: Flagship new topic: causal identification for compositional treatments on a simplex. Characterize when a reported 'effect of increasing component j' is a well-defined causal estimand: because treatment shares sum to one, every component increase requires a substitution rule over the remaining components. The proposed kernel is an exact reallocation-invariance theorem: a component-effect functional is nonparametrically identified from observational or randomized mixture data if and only if all admissible substitution paths have the same path derivative of the dose-response surface; otherwise only path-specific reallocation effects are identified. Include a finite three-component witness where two substitution paths have opposite signed component effects despite the same observed mixture-response law, plus a sharp simplex-gradient condition recovering standard continuous-treatment dose-response as the unconstrained limit. Position against continuous-treatment causal dose-response and compositional-data regression, not as an upgrade of any banked topic.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The Stage -1.1 catalogue scan reports no prior CausalSmith proposal on compositional treatments. The closest entries are pid_dose_response_iv, concerning continuous-dose derivative signs under cross-dose coupling, and pid_dose_response_shape, concerning joint-law restrictions for dosewise claims. The revised proposal differs by treating the treatment as a simplex-valued composition and making the focal object the support-span collapse frontier for reallocation versions.","published_axis":"The closest published comparators are ArnoldBerrieTennantGilthorpe2020 and TomovaGilthorpeTennant2022 on compositional causal substitutions, VanderWeeleHernan2013 on multiple treatment versions, Piepel2006 on direction-dependent mixture slopes, and HiranoImbens2004/HinesDiazOrdazVansteelandt2026 on continuous-treatment derivatives. The v3 gap is the exact condition Π_{L_j(a)}γ_{-j}(a)=0 under which all admissible substitution versions collapse to one component effect; Piepel-style opposite-sign direction dependence is no longer claimed as novel."}
seed_list: Characterize a necessary-and-sufficient simplex-gradient condition under which all reallocations increasing component j define one component causal effect.
Give a support-local identification theorem distinguishing identifiable simplex tangent directions from unsupported substitution policies.
Treat reallocation rules as treatment versions and characterize when version averaging yields one component estimand rather than a policy-indexed effect.
Derive the simplex tangent-space Riesz representer and efficient influence function for rule-specific and rule-invariant average derivative effects.
Construct sharp sensitivity bounds for substitution-direction effects under high-dimensional overlap failure and unmeasured confounding.
Compare component-slope, Scheffe, and Cox mixture coefficients by a causal non-representability theorem for invariant component effects.
Build a finite three-component open-class witness where two substitution paths have opposite signed causal derivatives under the same mixture-response surface.
Recover scalar continuous-treatment dose-response as the sharp unconstrained limit of simplex component effects under displaced-gradient equality.
Give a testable gradient-equality restriction for applied compositional regressions to certify when reported component coefficients are rule-invariant.
Formulate log-ratio and tangent-coordinate versions of substitution effects and prove their equivalence frontier for local causal derivatives.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize a necessary-and-sufficient simplex-gradient condition under which all reallocations increasing component j define one component causal effect.

anchor_paper.author: Arnold, Berrie, Tennant & Gilthorpe; Tomova, Gilthorpe & Tennant; Piepel
anchor_paper.venue: International Journal of Epidemiology / AJCN / Journal of Applied Statistics
anchor_paper.claim: Fixed-total compositional effects are joint relative/substitution effects and mixture slopes depend on direction/reference.
anchor_paper.location: Arnold 2020 p.1312; Tomova 2022 abstract/conclusions; Piepel 2006 abstract
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: "any increase in one must be accompanied by an equal and opposite decrease in the other"

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens no assumption; surprise is an iff frontier plus opposite-sign witness for the same response law.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from compositional regression because gradients on a simplex already live in a tangent space.
seed_details.rebuttal: Compositional regression names coordinate constraints; it does not give a causal iff condition for version-invariant component effects or a nonidentification boundary.

adversarial_referee.instance: Headline might lack a named focal object beyond a condition.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The focal object is the non-j simplex-gradient spread rho_j(a), computed in Exhibit 9.1.

adversarial_referee.instance: Necessity could be treated as obvious linear algebra.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The necessity side is explicitly tied to all admissible version paths and representation invariance in R^K/span(1).

adversarial_referee.instance: Three-component witness could be hand-tuned at one law.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The witness has relative-open support and nonzero gradients; an open-neighborhood promotion is named in the proof route.

seed_details.one_line: Give a support-local identification theorem distinguishing identifiable simplex tangent directions from unsupported substitution policies.

anchor_paper.author: Antonelli & Zigler; Huang, Dong, Lin & Antonelli; Hirano & Imbens
anchor_paper.venue: American Journal of Epidemiology / arXiv / book chapter
anchor_paper.claim: Mixture interventions can require unsupported mixture values; continuous-treatment GPS needs overlap.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Support extension is stronger only if it yields a sharp admissible-direction frontier; otherwise it is routine overlap restatement.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from continuous-treatment positivity because tangent directions are just local support.
seed_details.rebuttal: The simplex constraint couples coordinates, so coordinate support is insufficient; the proposed object is the admissible tangent cone.

seed_details.one_line: Treat reallocation rules as treatment versions and characterize when version averaging yields one component estimand rather than a policy-indexed effect.

anchor_paper.author: VanderWeele & Hernan; Chiu & Wen; Diaz
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of Causal Inference / Statistics in Medicine / JASA
anchor_paper.claim: Version or policy distributions define effects once specified; they do not decide when versions are equivalent.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Multiple-version extension is stronger only if it gives an equivalence relation over rules, not another specified policy effect.
seed_details.referee_objection: VanderWeele and Hernan already solve this by specifying version distributions.
seed_details.rebuttal: They identify effects after choosing a distribution; this seed asks when all distributions over simplex reallocations are effect-equivalent.

seed_details.one_line: Derive the simplex tangent-space Riesz representer and efficient influence function for rule-specific and rule-invariant average derivative effects.

anchor_paper.author: Hines, Diaz-Ordaz & Vansteelandt; Cattaneo; Aitchison & Bacon-Shone
anchor_paper.claim: Average derivative and multivalued-treatment efficiency results do not handle singular simplex share laws.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Estimator extension is stronger only if the Riesz representer changes because of the zero-sum tangent space.
seed_details.referee_objection: This follows from projecting Euclidean ADE influence functions onto the simplex tangent space.
seed_details.rebuttal: Projection alone misses boundary local-overlap and rule-invariance testing; still less clean than the exact-ID iff seed.

seed_details.one_line: Construct sharp sensitivity bounds for substitution-direction effects under high-dimensional overlap failure and unmeasured confounding.

anchor_paper.author: D'Amour, Ding, Feller, Lei & Sekhon; Zheng, D'Amour & Franks; D'Amour
anchor_paper.claim: High-dimensional multicomponent causal effects suffer overlap fragility and sensitivity to parameterization under confounding.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sensitivity seed needs L(delta), U(delta), and critical delta; otherwise sufficient-only sensitivity.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is ordinary multi-treatment sensitivity with a compositional reparameterization.
seed_details.rebuttal: A tangent-coordinate invariant bound could differ, but the current seed lacks the exact computable object.

seed_details.one_line: Compare component-slope, Scheffe, and Cox mixture coefficients by a causal non-representability theorem for invariant component effects.

anchor_paper.author: Piepel
anchor_paper.claim: Component-slope, Scheffe, and Cox parameterizations assign different meanings to mixture coefficients.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Comparator decomposition is stronger only if it proves no coefficient represents the invariant estimand outside the gradient-equality class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a statistical parameterization comparison already in Piepel.
seed_details.rebuttal: The causal non-representability clause is new, but narrower than the main iff seed.

seed_details.one_line: Build a finite three-component open-class witness where two substitution paths have opposite signed causal derivatives under the same mixture-response surface.

anchor_paper.author: Tomova, Gilthorpe & Tennant; Piepel
anchor_paper.claim: Substitution estimands and component slopes vary with the displaced component or direction.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample is stronger only if promoted beyond one hand-tuned law; open support witness supplies that.
seed_details.referee_objection: A sign flip is expected once different directions are chosen.
seed_details.rebuttal: As a standalone seed it is not flagship; as Exhibit 9.1 it supports the iff theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Recover scalar continuous-treatment dose-response as the sharp unconstrained limit of simplex component effects under displaced-gradient equality.

anchor_paper.author: Hirano & Imbens; Hines, Diaz-Ordaz & Vansteelandt
anchor_paper.claim: Continuous-treatment dose-response and average derivative effects use Euclidean variation, not simplex-constrained substitution.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Limit theorem is stronger only with a sharp necessary condition; otherwise a routine constrained derivative.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just restricting an ambient gradient to a tangent space.
seed_details.rebuttal: The sharp displaced-gradient equality is useful as a sanity corollary, not enough as the flagship kernel.

seed_details.one_line: Give a testable gradient-equality restriction for applied compositional regressions to certify when reported component coefficients are rule-invariant.

anchor_paper.author: Arnold, Berrie, Tennant & Gilthorpe; Tomova, Gilthorpe & Tennant
anchor_paper.claim: Applied compositional analyses must state the relative effect being sought and modeled.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Testable restriction seed is weaker than identification unless paired with exact iff and support conditions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an empirical diagnostic rather than a theorem-level contribution.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is useful as a downstream implication of the main iff, not as the chosen seed.

seed_details.one_line: Formulate log-ratio and tangent-coordinate versions of substitution effects and prove their equivalence frontier for local causal derivatives.

anchor_paper.author: Aitchison & Bacon-Shone; Hines, Diaz-Ordaz & Vansteelandt
anchor_paper.claim: Log-contrast models encode zero-sum compositional restrictions, while ADE work uses continuous-treatment derivatives.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Bridge seed is stronger only if equivalence fails at boundary or under different metrics; otherwise coordinate algebra.
seed_details.referee_objection: Log-ratio and tangent coordinates are smooth reparameterizations on the interior.
seed_details.rebuttal: The boundary and causal-rule index might matter, but current version risks being definitional.

proposed_from.literature_map: Scheffe/Cox/Piepel mixture models make component slopes direction- or reference-dependent. Aitchison and Bacon-Shone impose zero-sum log-contrast restrictions for compositional regression. Arnold, Berrie, Tennant and Gilthorpe (2020, IJE) distinguish total and relative causal effects and state that fixed-total compositions identify relative effects only. Tomova, Gilthorpe and Tennant (2022, AJCN) separate direct substitutions, inadvertent substitutions, and average relative causal effects. VanderWeele and Hernan (2013, JCI) show that multiple treatment versions define different effects unless version distributions are specified. Hirano and Imbens (2004) and Cattaneo (2010, JoE) cover continuous/multivalued treatment identification but not singular simplex-valued shares. Hines, Diaz-Ordaz and Vansteelandt (2026, Biometrika) treat average derivative effects under local overlap, not simplex tangent derivatives. Antonelli and Zigler (2024, AJE) and Huang et al. (2026, arXiv) emphasize support and positivity failures for mixture interventions. D'Amour et al. (2021, JoE) and Zheng et al. (2022, AISTATS) motivate overlap and partial-ID concerns for high-dimensional multicomponent treatments. The live gap is when all policies increasing one component collapse to one causal estimand.

banked_reason: Stopped early after Stage -0.5 angle0 v3 still REVISE at field tier: the compositional-treatment topic has a clear repo gap, but the proposed identification kernel remains elementary support-span/gradient geometry; other Stage -1.1 angles appeared field-tier unless converted into an estimator/inference paper.
